stories.headline: March NFP +178K Crushes 59K Estimate — Soft Wages the Market's Friday Gift
stories.body: March Non-Farm Payrolls blew past expectations at +178,000 vs. a 59,000 consensus. Average Hourly Earnings rose just +0.2% MoM / +3.5% YoY — well below the expected 3.7% and the slowest annual wage growth since May 2021. Strong hiring + soft wages is the Fed's best-case scenario: validates holding rates without stoking inflation fear. Markets were closed for Good Friday — equity futures are expected to gap higher Monday.

stories.headline: Last Close (Apr 2): S&P 6,582 +0.11% — Wild Session on Iran Reversal
stories.body: Thursday's session opened sharply lower on Trump's "war continues for weeks" remarks, then reversed mid-session after Iranian state media reported Hormuz monitoring talks in Oman. S&P 500 finished nearly flat at 6,582 (+0.11%), Nasdaq +0.18%, Dow −0.13%. The 10Y yield pulled back to 4.31% after touching 4.38% intraday. U.S. markets closed Friday for Good Friday.

stories.headline: Iran–Oman Working on Hormuz Monitoring Protocol — Oil War Premium Intact
stories.body: President Trump said Thursday the Iran conflict would last "for weeks," but Iranian state media reported Tehran is working with Oman on a protocol to monitor ship passage through the Strait of Hormuz. Brent crude hit $111.69 intraday before trimming gains. Oil futures now price a 98% probability of WTI reaching $105+ in 2026. A formal monitoring agreement, if reached, could partially restore tanker traffic without a full ceasefire.

stories.headline: Rate Hike Probability Hit 52% Thursday — Soft NFP Wages Should Reverse That Monday
stories.body: Oil-driven inflation fears pushed 2026 rate hike probability to 52% in futures markets Thursday. Friday's AHE print at +3.5% YoY should pull that back at Monday's open. The FOMC meets April 28–29; strong hiring + cooling wages gives the Fed cover to hold without hawkish guidance. June is effectively off the table.

stories.headline: Gold Holds $4,675; Any Hormuz Deal Would Be the Fed's Biggest Disinflationary Gift
stories.body: Gold held at $4,675/oz — well off the January record of $5,111 but supported by the geopolitical bid. A credible Hormuz resolution could send crude down $15–20/barrel overnight, which would be the single most powerful disinflationary shock available to the Fed in 2026 and would almost certainly price out any remaining hike expectations.

stories.headline: Tariff Burden: $1,500/Household in 2026; U.S.–EU Framework Talks Stall
stories.body: The post-SCOTUS tariff landscape (sector-specific levies remain after IEEPA ruling) is costing the average household an estimated $1,500 this year per Yale Budget Lab. U.S.–EU framework trade talks reportedly stalled this week over digital services tax provisions — a persistent uncertainty for European multinationals with U.S. exposure heading into Q2 earnings.

stories.headline: Men's Final Four Tonight in Indianapolis: UConn vs. Illinois, Arizona vs. Michigan
stories.body: The men's Final Four tips off tonight at Lucas Oil Stadium in Indianapolis. No. 2 UConn faces No. 3 Illinois in the early game; No. 1 Arizona takes on No. 1 Michigan in the nightcap. Championship Monday is April 6. On the women's side Friday night, South Carolina eliminated undefeated UConn 62-48, ending the Huskies' 38-0 run. South Carolina faces UCLA in Monday's women's title game.

stories.headline: Luka Doncic Out for Season With Grade 2 Hamstring Tear — Lakers Playoff Hopes in Doubt
stories.body: The Lakers announced Friday that Luka Doncic will miss the remainder of the regular season after suffering a Grade 2 left hamstring tear in Thursday's 139-97 blowout loss to OKC. Grade 2 tears typically require 4-6 weeks. The Lakers have clinched a playoff spot but now face a brutal first-round draw without their star. OKC (61-16) owns the top seed in the West. Regular season ends April 12; playoffs begin April 18.

stories.headline: NFL Annual Meeting Wraps: Onside Kick Rule Changed, Kirk Cousins to Raiders, Gilmore Retires
stories.body: The NFL's annual meeting in Phoenix concluded with several notable moves. Teams voted to allow onside kicks at any point in a game (previously restricted to the fourth quarter). Kirk Cousins agreed to terms with the Las Vegas Raiders. Five-time Pro Bowl CB Stephon Gilmore announced his retirement after 13 seasons. The Atlanta Falcons unveiled new primary uniforms. NFL Draft is April 23-25 in Pittsburgh.

stories.headline: Spring Game Season: Lane Kiffin Arrives at LSU, Big Ten Holds Power With 3 Straight Titles
stories.body: Spring practice is in full swing across the country with 33 FBS programs breaking in new head coaches. LSU's biggest offseason move: Lane Kiffin hired away from Ole Miss after leading them to the CFP last season. The Big Ten has won three consecutive national championships, with ESPN's initial SP+ rankings reflecting a power shift from the SEC. UW's spring game is April 26 at Husky Stadium under HC Jedd Fisch.

sports.detail: Beat Minnesota Thursday. Jenkins 26 pts; Duren 22 pts/14 reb. Cade Cunningham out. Solidifying #1 East seed. Playoff bracket drops April 19.
Shut out by Yankees — Max Fried 7 IP, 0 ER. Sit 2–2. Off Good Friday. Next: vs. Angels, Sat April 4, T-Mobile Park.
Draft April 23–25, Pittsburgh. Picks: #32, 64, 96, 188. Added Rashid Shaheed (WR/ST). Mock consensus: CB or EDGE at #32.
Won Round 1 72–54 vs SDSU — Avery Howell 30 pts. Lost to #3 TCU in Round of 32 OT. First March win since 2017.
Week 2 spring camp under HC Jedd Fisch. New offensive system install. Spring game April 26 at Husky Stadium.

watchlist: Monday open — markets react to NFP +178K / AHE +3.5% YoY (dovish read)
FOMC April 28–29 — hike odds at 52% Thu; soft wages should pull back
Oman–Iran Hormuz protocol — any deal moves oil ±$10–15
Mariners vs. Angels — Sat April 4, T-Mobile Park
Seahawks NFL Draft — April 23, Pittsburgh, pick #32

deepDive.title: How the Fed Actually Signals a Policy Change
content: The Federal Reserve doesn't just flip a switch on rates — it telegraphs moves months in advance through a layered communication system that markets have learned to decode in real time. Understanding the signal chain matters as much as watching the fed funds rate itself, because by the time the FOMC votes, the market has usually priced the outcome with 90%+ probability.

The first layer is the FOMC statement itself — a tightly worded document where single-word changes carry weight. When the Fed dropped the phrase 'further increases may be appropriate' in January 2024 and replaced it with language about 'any additional policy firming,' every trading desk in New York noticed. The SEP (Summary of Economic Projections), released four times a year alongside the dot plot, shows where each anonymous official sees rates in 12, 24, and 36 months. The dot plot doesn't bind anyone — it reflects conditions at the time — but median dots reliably shape market expectations for the next quarter.

The second layer is the Chair's press conference. Powell's post-meeting Q&A is parsed word-by-word; his tone, hedge phrases, and off-script moments often move markets more than the written statement. Formal speeches at venues like the Economic Club of New York or the Jackson Hole Symposium are drafted carefully and function as policy pre-announcements. When Powell used the word 'time' at Jackson Hole in August 2022 — specifically 'it is time to bring down inflation' — the S&P dropped 3.4% that afternoon. One word.

The third layer is Fed regional presidents and governors, who give speeches between meetings. Markets weight their comments by whether they are voting FOMC members that year and by their known position on the hawk-dove spectrum. A surprise hawkish comment from a perceived dove (like Chicago Fed President Goolsbee in early 2024) moves markets more than the same comment from an established hawk. The Atlanta Fed's GDPNow tracker and the New York Fed's Nowcast are the real-time economic inputs the market uses to anticipate whether upcoming data will reinforce or contradict existing Fed signals. With the FOMC meeting April 28–29, and today's NFP softening the wage outlook, the signal chain right now points to hold — with the next real fork in the road being June's data window.
